surface_or_claim: The public registry can be downloaded for browser/device execution.
evidence: `src/router.js` implements unauthenticated `GET /v1/registry/export` and exports public concept source plus metadata.
code_truth: The route is real and intended for the on-device payload.
risk: This is a useful browser-runtime foundation if execution and cache claims are narrowed.
recommended_action: Keep the route, add schema tests, and document source exposure as public-by-design.

surface_or_claim: The live public export contains runnable public recipes with hashes.
evidence: Live `https://kolm.ai/v1/registry/export` returned `spec=rs-1`, `recipes_n=26`, a registry hash, and 26 rows with `source_hash`.
code_truth: Live export is populated with source hashes.
risk: The trust-gate fallback has data today, but not bundle signatures.
recommended_action: Keep source hashes required and add signature/revocation metadata before calling the bundle signed.

surface_or_claim: The service worker file is syntactically valid.
evidence: `node --check .\public\sw.js` passed locally; live `https://kolm.ai/sw.js` fetched successfully and matched the local logic.
code_truth: The service worker script itself parses.
risk: A valid SW helps PWA readiness, but does not prove full offline runtime.
recommended_action: Keep syntax checks and add behavior tests for installed offline mode.

surface_or_claim: The device page registers a service worker and links a manifest.
evidence: `public/device.html` links `/manifest.json` and calls `navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js')`.
code_truth: PWA wiring is present at the HTML level.
risk: Registration success can be mistaken for offline readiness.
recommended_action: Change status text to distinguish registration from cache completeness.

surface_or_claim: Public registry strings are sanitized before rendering.
evidence: `src/registry.js` sanitizes concept names, descriptions, and tags; `/device` also has an `esc()` helper before innerHTML use.
code_truth: There is a real defense-in-depth path for public registry display.
risk: Public source still needs trust controls, but name/description rendering is not raw.
recommended_action: Keep sanitizer tests and avoid bypassing `esc()` in device rows.

surface_or_claim: The Node artifact recipe path has a sandbox warning and safety scan.
evidence: `src/verifier.js` uses `node:vm`, blocks dangerous tokens, and says production should harden with isolated-vm or wasmtime.
code_truth: The local artifact runtime is not claiming `node:vm` as a hard boundary in code comments.
risk: This is an honest implementation note that should guide browser-worker language too.
recommended_action: Keep the caveat in public docs and continue sandbox hardening.

surface_or_claim: Static tests already ban some unsupported mobile/runtime phrasing.
evidence: `tests/site.test.js` forbids `Mobile SDK` and `zero runtime egress` in public pages.
code_truth: Some broad unsupported claims are guarded.
risk: The test policy is useful but misses the actual browser asset failures.
recommended_action: Extend forbidden-claim policy with runnable-asset gates.

surface_or_claim: `/sdk.js` is importable by browser users.
evidence: `node --check .\public\sdk.js` fails at `_now()` on an invalid ternary-like expression.
code_truth: The local browser SDK does not parse.
risk: Any page or customer importing `/sdk.js` can fail before runtime code runs.
recommended_action: Fix the syntax corruption and require `node --check public/sdk.js` in CI.

surface_or_claim: The current content-addressed SDK is safe to pin.
evidence: `public/sdk-current.json` points to `/sdk-c102c349da28.js`; `node --check .\public\sdk-c102c349da28.js` fails on the same `_now()` expression.
code_truth: The current pinned browser SDK is broken locally.
risk: Pinned users can be locked to a non-parsing asset.
recommended_action: Regenerate only after syntax checks and mark broken versions deprecated in the manifest.

surface_or_claim: Older versioned SDK assets provide a fallback.
evidence: `node --check` failed for `sdk-4d6d60e67927.js` and `sdk-ef22b94a7a38.js`.
code_truth: All local versioned SDK files checked in this pass are broken.
risk: Rollback to an older local asset may not help users.
recommended_action: Keep a known-good asset and add version-manifest health fields.

surface_or_claim: SDK versioning prevents invalid releases.
evidence: `scripts/build-sdk-version.js` copies `public/sdk.js`, computes hashes, and writes manifests without syntax validation.
code_truth: A broken SDK can be stamped as current.
risk: The release process can publish non-runnable SDK assets.
recommended_action: Run syntax/browser smoke checks before writing `sdk-current.json`.

surface_or_claim: `recipe-worker.js` runs recipe code in a browser Worker sandbox.
evidence: `node --check .\public\recipe-worker.js` fails at `let fn = sh - compiled.get(sh) : null`; live worker failed the same check.
code_truth: The worker does not parse.
risk: The intended sandbox cannot initialize, breaking local browser execution.
recommended_action: Fix worker syntax and add worker smoke tests.

surface_or_claim: The `/device` page can hydrate and run recipes.
evidence: Extracted `public/device.html` module script failed `node --check`; live `/device` contains the same broken source-hash and status ternary lines.
code_truth: The device page module does not parse.
risk: The public demo can fail before registry hydration begins.
recommended_action: Fix module syntax and make module parsing part of `tests/site.test.js`.

surface_or_claim: Public script parse tests catch browser runtime regressions.
evidence: `tests/site.test.js` skips module scripts and external scripts, so it missed `/device`, `/sdk.js`, versioned SDKs, and `recipe-worker.js`.
code_truth: The current site test does not cover the shipped browser runtime surface.
risk: Broken browser assets can ship while root tests pass.
recommended_action: Parse module scripts and run `node --check` on external runtime assets.

surface_or_claim: Local and deployed browser assets differ enough that local evidence is stale.
evidence: Live `/device`, `/sw.js`, `/recipe-worker.js`, and `sdk-current.json` were fetched; the live device/worker assets showed the same broken lines and current SDK pointer as local.
code_truth: The live site currently mirrors the broken browser runtime enough to treat this as deployed risk.
risk: Prospects can encounter the failure now, not just in local development.
recommended_action: Deploy a fixed browser bundle or temporarily remove runnable-device claims.

surface_or_claim: After first load, the next visit hits no network.
evidence: `public/sdk.js` reads cache but still fetches `/v1/registry/export` first; `public/sw.js` stale-revalidates the registry when cached.
code_truth: Actual behavior is network-first with cached fallback, plus background revalidation.
risk: The page overstates offline/no-network behavior and can confuse compliance buyers.
recommended_action: Change wording to cached fallback or implement cache-first offline mode with explicit sync.

surface_or_claim: The PWA install cache includes every runtime dependency.
evidence: `PRECACHE` contains `/device`, `/styles.css`, `/sdk.js`, `/v1/registry/export`, and `/manifest.json`, but not `/recipe-worker.js` or `/brand-refresh.css`.
code_truth: Install-time cache is incomplete for the device page and SDK worker path.
risk: An installed app can be offline while a required worker or stylesheet is missing.
recommended_action: Precache the worker and all device dependencies, then verify offline reload.

surface_or_claim: Adding `/device` to the home screen opens the device runtime.
evidence: `public/device.html` links `/manifest.json`; that manifest has `start_url: '/'`.
code_truth: The installed PWA start target is the homepage, not `/device`.
risk: The install claim can produce the wrong first screen.
recommended_action: Use a device-specific manifest or set `start_url` to `/device` for this app.

surface_or_claim: `Offline-ready: yes` proves the app can run offline.
evidence: The page sets `sOffline` to `yes` when service-worker registration succeeds; it does not verify cache entries or offline reload.
code_truth: The UI reports registration, not offline readiness.
risk: Users can trust an unverified status indicator.
recommended_action: Check cache completeness and an offline fetch path before showing ready.

surface_or_claim: The device page accurately counts network calls.
evidence: `sNetwork` is initialized as `0` and set to `1 (registry)` after load; the code does not instrument fetch, worker script loads, CSS, manifest, or SW requests.
code_truth: The counter is hardcoded status text, not observed telemetry.
risk: The UI can undercount network activity while making no-network claims.
recommended_action: Instrument network activity or remove the counter.

surface_or_claim: The browser trust gate verifies signed public registry metadata.
evidence: Live export had no top-level `signature` and no recipe-level `signature`; `/device` falls back to `source_hash` verification when no HMAC is present.
code_truth: The browser export is hash-checked, not signed as a bundle.
risk: Tamper evidence is weaker than the HMAC trust-root copy implies.
recommended_action: Emit signed registry envelopes or rewrite the trust-gate copy to source-hash verification.

surface_or_claim: Browser local runs return signed receipts.
evidence: `public/sdk.js` constructs a local receipt object with hashes and runtime fields; no HMAC or signature is added, and `verifyReceipt()` posts to `/v1/receipts/verify`.
code_truth: Browser run receipts are unsigned local metadata.
risk: Audit and compliance language can overstate browser proof.
recommended_action: Call them metadata until signed per-call browser receipts exist.

surface_or_claim: Public recipe source never reaches a main-thread `Function`.
evidence: `public/sdk.js` intends worker compilation, but also has a last-resort fallback that compiles `r.meta.source` with `new Function` on the main thread.
code_truth: Worker isolation is not unconditional even after syntax is fixed.
risk: A missing Worker path can expand the public-source execution boundary.
recommended_action: Disable fallback for untrusted public registry rows or gate it behind explicit unsafe mode.

surface_or_claim: The browser worker prevents recipe network egress.
evidence: `recipe-worker.js` deletes several worker globals, but the worker currently does not parse; timeouts are cooperative within the worker function.
code_truth: The intended lockdown is not runnable today and is not a full hard sandbox claim.
risk: Network/privacy claims need browser-level proof, not just code comments.
recommended_action: Fix worker, add malicious recipe tests, and document the residual boundary.

surface_or_claim: The same import works in Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, and browsers.
evidence: The device code snippet claims those runtimes; `public/sdk.js` uses browser Worker, `localStorage`, `import.meta.url`, and browser-specific cache behavior, and currently has syntax errors.
code_truth: The current SDK is browser-oriented and broken before cross-runtime support can be evaluated.
risk: Runtime support claims are too broad.
recommended_action: Split browser SDK from server/edge SDKs and add runtime matrix tests.

surface_or_claim: Browser SDK `wrap()` can authenticate verified calls.
evidence: The constructor stores `apiKey`, but `wrap()` builds the auth header from `self.key`; the same block also has corrupted ternary syntax.
code_truth: Authenticated wrap calls can omit the intended API key even after syntax is fixed.
risk: Verified inference onboarding can fail silently or return auth errors.
recommended_action: Use `self.apiKey`, test wrap auth, and fix syntax.

surface_or_claim: Every recipe below runs locally, in microseconds, with no network call.
evidence: The page uses this phrasing; runtime assets fail syntax checks, worker is broken, and the network counter is not observed telemetry.
code_truth: The claim is currently false for the shipped browser path.
risk: This can damage trust because the demo itself is the proof asset.
recommended_action: Replace with experimental wording until browser smoke passes.

surface_or_claim: Runtime is on-device and users run it offline.
evidence: `public/security.html` says users run offline and receipts stay on device, while also caveating native offline runtimes as pending contract review.
code_truth: Security copy mixes a useful narrower caveat with broader offline wording.
risk: Enterprise buyers may read the broader line as shipped local browser/native proof.
recommended_action: Thread the caveat through all security/device pages.

surface_or_claim: PHI can stay local with zero inference egress and primary offline mode.
evidence: `public/articles/hipaa-on-device.html` includes caveats about validating mature local deployments, but also lists inference egress as `0 bytes` and offline mode as primary.
code_truth: The article is partially caveated but still stronger than current browser/native proof.
risk: Regulated buyers need implementation evidence, not aspirational architecture.
recommended_action: Label healthcare runtime as target architecture until browser/native proof is complete.

surface_or_claim: RS-1 artifacts run on commodity devices with no network and offline verification.
evidence: `public/whitepaper.html` has a v0.1 implementation-status caveat for model/adapter/index, but also states portability and offline operation as RS-1 goals.
code_truth: The spec narrative is ahead of the current browser SDK and native runtime proof.
risk: Spec language can be read as shipped guarantee rather than target contract.
recommended_action: Separate normative future contract from v0.1 implementation status in public pages.
